title: Correlation Is Not Enough: Configurational Outcome Modelling (COM) Reveals Hidden Causal Structures in Complex Systems — A Next-Generation Alternative to Correlation-Only Inference
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"xos09","text":"This protocol describes the Configurational Influence Assessment Method (CIAS), a non-linear behavioural measurement procedure designed to quantify how Context, Situational Pressure, and Trait variables interact within real or imagined situations to influence a specified dependent variable. Unlike traditional correlational or Likert-based approaches that measure simple co-occurrence, CIAS captures the dynamic configurational effect that emerges when independent variables are simultaneously present. Participants rate triad-grouped items on a continuous 0–100 scale, enabling fractional precision and preserving contribution asymmetry. Raw values are calibrated into fuzzy set membership scores using pre-defined anchor points (0.0, 0.5, 1.0) prior to set-theoretic analysis. Boolean minimization is used to determine necessity, sufficiency, and solution coverage. This method accommodates formative indicator logic, where lower internal consistency is expected due to conceptual non-redundancy. The protocol includes operator instructions, quality-control safeguards, stopping rules, and data structure requirements to ensure reproducibility. CIAS is suitable for research in decision-making, leadership, organizational behaviour, clinical profiling, and cognitive flexibility, and is optimized for publication, replication, and transparent dissemination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b4dxh","text":"- Familiarity with fsQCA concepts (necessity/sufficiency, calibration)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4umw2","text":"- Experience with membership calibration and anchoring justification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17stv","text":"- Training in triad administration and quality-control detection (stereotyped patterns, rounding bias)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"769sq","text":"All materials must be identical across researchers to ensure reproducibility. Researchers must specify software version and calibration command settings in the dissemination statement.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"wvusu","text":"- Continuous 0–100 rating interface (digital preferred)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8dp9d","text":"- The full item set representing each configurational element (Context, Situational Pressure, Trait)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hwwlz","text":"- Data entry template (CSV or XLSX)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"khcft","text":"- Software capable of performing fsQCA (e.g., fsQCA 3.1, R package \"QCA\", or Tosmana)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"upuhj","text":"- Instructions sheet for participants","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1pwfu","text":"- IRB-approved consent form","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lfjq","text":"- Scoring sheet for item-triad groupings","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Define the N distinct elements (e.g., Context, Situational Pressure, Trait) that form the configuration and the Dependent Variable (DV). A clearly defined theoretical model (e.g., a Triad, Quadrad).
Develop a full item set to represent each element. Crucially, split the total item pool into at least two independent, theoretically parallel sets. Parallel item sets ready for administration.

Recruit Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) N Approximate 30 and conduct two tasks: Item Understandability: High Interclass Correlation (ICC) (e.g., ICC = 0.785). Distinctiveness: High Mean Likert Rating (≥ 4.0 out of 5) and a statistically significant RM-ANOVA.

Administer the full instrument to a large sample (N = 100+) and calculate Cronbach's alpha for the items within each single element (IV). Expected Failure (Theoretical Success): A low α (e.g., α approx. 0.129) is required, confirming the elements are distinct formative indicators.

Calculate the two configurational coefficients using the composite scores from the main sample. Intra-Configuration Coherence (r1): High value (e.g., 0.80), proving systematic alignment within the configuration. Global Conceptual Stability (r2): High value (e.g., 0.80), correlating Set A vs. Set B total scores, proving stability across item sets.
Mandatory Check: Confirm that Phase I has been successfully completed, with (r1) and (r2) achieving high values. CTRM Validation Report must show (r1) and (r2) are high.

Group the related items of each configurational element into triads (e.g., Context Item, Situational Pressure Item, Trait Item) under shared conceptual domains (e.g., Integrative Coherence). Minimum 6 triads per participant.

Present real or imagined situations and instruct participants to score each item using the free continuous scale of 0 (not applicable at all) to 100 (perfectly applicable). The Dependent Variable (DV) is also scored on this 0-100 scale. Raw continuous decimal values recorded for all IVs and the DV.

Discontinue administration if fatigue or identical/stereotyped patterns occur (e.g., e20% triads with identical values). Exclude data if quality control metrics are violated. High-quality data set of raw 0-100 scores from N ≥25 participants.

Transform all raw 0-100 scores (for both IVs and DV) into 0.0-1.0 fuzzy membership scores using an S-shaped function. The three anchor points must be set: Full Non-Membership: 5 to 0.0; Crossover Point:50 to 0.5. Full Membership: 95 to 1.0
Test if any single IV or combination of IVs is a necessary condition for the DV (Outcome). Necessity Consistency Score 0.90 indicates the outcome almost never occurs without that condition.

Process data into a Truth Table and use Boolean minimization (Quine-McCluskey) to generate the most parsimonious solution (combinations of IVs sufficient for the outcome). Solution Term (e.g., High Context AND High Trait) and Solution Consistency
The distinct, non-linear contribution is quantified by the key fsQCA metrics. Solution Consistency 0.80 (reliably sufficient) and Solution Coverage 0.50 (empirical relevance).
creator: Ramesh Kumar G S
